title: The U.S. Army Designed a Program to Escape Time and Space. Then They Buried It.
title_es: El Ejército de EE. UU. diseñó un programa para escapar del tiempo y el espacio. Luego lo enterró.
description: In 1983, Lt. Col. Wayne McDonnell was tasked with evaluating the Monroe Institute's Gateway Process — a training program for hemispheric synchronization and out-of-body states. His 28-page report referenced quantum mechanics, holographic universe theory, and consciousness research. It concluded the program worked. Then it was classified for 20 years, with one page missing.
description_es: En 1983, el Tte. Cor. Wayne McDonnell fue asignado a evaluar el Proceso Gateway del Instituto Monroe — un programa de entrenamiento para sincronización hemisférica y estados fuera del cuerpo. Su reporte de 28 páginas citó mecánica cuántica, teoría del universo holográfico e investigación de la consciencia. Concluyó que el programa funcionaba. Luego fue clasificado por 20 años, con una página faltante.
author: Alejandro del Palacio
keywords: what is the CIA Gateway Process
targetQuery: what is the CIA Gateway Process
imageAlt: Declassified-style brown manila folder labeled CIA-RDP96-00788R002000280003-7 partially open on a 1970s government desk under a single fluorescent lamp
imagePrompt: Retrofuturist 1970s cinematic still photograph. Dim windowless government office dominates 70 percent of the frame in deep desaturated brown and matte black with heavy 35mm grain. Center: a worn manila CIA folder stamped CONFIDENTIAL in red ink, partially open to reveal a single typewritten page with the header GATEWAY PROCESS · ARMY INTEL · TOP SECRET. The exposed page shows technical diagrams of brain waves and binaural frequencies rendered in faded blueprint cyan, with one paragraph circled in red. A tarnished brass desk lamp casts an amber pool of light onto the document; the rest of the desk is in deep shadow. Vintage steel pen, half-empty coffee cup, and a black rotary phone in soft focus background. The folder + page + brass lamp are the ONLY chromatic zone — desaturated cream, blueprint cyan, red stamp, amber lamplight breaking out of the matte black office. Heavy 35mm film grain, anamorphic lens flare, kodak ektachrome retro slide aesthetic. Mood: All The President's Men + Three Days of the Condor + The Conversation. No recognizable face. Mysterious forensic government-archive aesthetic. Photographic NOT painterly NOT illustrated.

howto.name: Gateway Process — Focus Levels Progression
howto.description: The Monroe Institute's Hemi-Sync-guided consciousness state progression, analyzed by U.S. Army INSCOM in 1983 and classified for 20 years. Sequence of brainwave-entrainment states built on binaural-beat technology.
tools: Audio source capable of stereo playback
supply: Hemi-Sync audio track (official Monroe Institute program)
howto.safety: Do not practice while driving or operating machinery. Discontinue if experiencing seizures, severe anxiety, or dissociative episodes. Consult a physician if you have epilepsy or a history of psychiatric conditions.

steps.name: Focus 10 — Mind Awake, Body Asleep
steps.text: Settle into a reclined posture with stereo headphones. Play the Focus 10 Hemi-Sync track. Let the body enter deep physical relaxation while conscious awareness remains alert. Do not force sleep; simply let the binaural frequencies entrain the brainwave pattern. Session 30-45 minutes.

steps.name: Focus 12 — Expanded Awareness
steps.text: From Focus 10, transition to the Focus 12 track. Awareness expands outward beyond the sense of the physical body. Early remote-viewing perception may emerge. Allow perception to extend without grasping at content.

steps.name: Focus 15 — No Time
steps.text: From Focus 12, transition to Focus 15. Linear-time perception drops; past and future co-exist with the present. Past-life exploration and precognitive perception are reported at this level.

steps.name: Focus 21 — The Bridge
steps.text: The edge of time-space. Described in Monroe Institute materials as the bridge between physical reality and non-physical dimensions. Reached only after sustained practice of Focus 10, 12, and 15.

steps.name: Integration
steps.text: Return gradually to Focus 10 and then to ordinary waking consciousness. Journal the session immediately. Record sensory content, duration estimates, any remote-viewing targets, and physiological state.

seoTitle: CIA Gateway Process: Hemi-Sync, Monroe Institute, Declassified Report
seoTitle_es: Proceso Gateway CIA: Hemi-Sync, Monroe Institute, reporte desclasificado
seoDescription: Lt. Col. McDonnell's classified 1983 evaluation of the Monroe Institute's Hemi-Sync program — the technical analysis, focus levels, and the missing page 25.
seoDescription_es: La evaluación clasificada de 1983 del Tte. Cor. McDonnell sobre el programa Hemi-Sync del Monroe Institute — el análisis técnico, niveles de enfoque y la página 25 perdida.
bluf: The Gateway Process is a CIA-evaluated training program developed by the Monroe Institute for hemispheric synchronization and out-of-body states. In 1983, Lt. Col. Wayne McDonnell's 28-page evaluation report concluded the program worked, citing quantum mechanics and consciousness research. The report was classified for 20 years and declassified in 2003 — with one page (page 25) missing. That missing page remains classified.
bluf_es: El Proceso Gateway es un programa de entrenamiento evaluado por la CIA desarrollado por el Instituto Monroe para sincronización hemisférica y estados fuera del cuerpo. En 1983, el reporte de evaluación de 28 páginas del Tte. Cnel. Wayne McDonnell concluyó que el programa funcionaba, citando mecánica cuántica e investigación de consciencia. El reporte fue clasificado durante 20 años y desclasificado en 2003 — con una página (la 25) faltante. Esa página faltante sigue clasificada.
relatedTargetQueries: what is the CIA Gateway Process
